name: fail9-candidate-unit-claude
description: Recorded Claude -p outputs for the generic candidate-unit action IR prompt on the nine fail18 cases discussed during the semantic graph eval loop.
compiler_contract: Return exactly one strict JSON object for the input id. No markdown.
Treat evidence as authoritative scenario memory. Do not reject entities, events, products, or claims merely because they seem fictional, unfamiliar, or unsupported by outside knowledge.
Use only the evidence text plus ordinary language/type knowledge. Do not use web knowledge or plausibility checks.
First determine answer_variable: entity | action_obligation | event | semantic_type | scalar_value | duration.
Then determine individuation_policy: canonical_entity | action_obligation | event_instance | semantic_type | scalar_value | duration_sum.
Represent direct propositions using action(subject, object, verb). Use subject=user for the human whose memory is being queried.

Also emit typed facts when needed: event(event_id,event_type), participant(event_id,role,entity), attribute(node,key,value), isa(node,type), same_entity(a,b), duration(node,unit,value), time(node,value), provenance(fact_id,session_id,span).
Before computing the answer, build candidate_units: every plausible answer unit from every evidence session, with included|excluded|merged status and a terse reason.
If a relevant entity/event/artifact lacks a precise name, create a skolem object id from its supported description and provenance instead of dropping it.
Normalize recursively: normalized_action(S,O,V2) from action + verb_alias; typed_object(O,T2) from isa + subtype; canonical_object(O2) from same_entity chains.
Infer action satisfaction by meaning, not surface wording: evidence may satisfy a requested action predicate through synonyms, entailments, or result states.

If text establishes the user now possesses, controls, received, has, or added an object, that can satisfy an acquired/obtained predicate unless evidence says borrowed, rented, temporary, canceled, or not theirs.

If the question asks for an artifact kind, include physical/digital realizations of that artifact kind. A format/container descriptor does not exclude the object if ordinary type knowledge says it realizes the requested kind.
Choose the answer variable from the question, then aggregate only included candidate_units of that variable. Count answer units, not sentence mentions.
Merge mentions only when the selected individuation_policy says they are the same answer unit. Do not merge two different answer units just because they refer to related real-world objects.
For action_obligation policy, do not merge obligations merely because they involve related or exchanged objects; merge only if they are the same required action on the same obligation target.
For event_instance policy, people, roles, locations, and themes are attributes used for coreference and deduplication; they are not themselves counted unless the question asks for them.

For cross-session event coreference, default to same_entity when two event mentions share compatible event type plus a distinctive participant name or distinctive description, unless evidence explicitly says they are separate occasions.
A changed relationship label alone is not explicit evidence of a separate event; relationship labels can be noisy attributes.
For semantic_type policy, count distinct kinds/classes after normalizing subtypes and aliases, not mentions or containers.
For duration_sum policy, sum numeric durations over matching events/entities and preserve units.

Count only facts asserted about the user/history/state relevant to the question. Do not count assistant advice, recommendations, hypothetical examples, future plans, or generic possibilities unless the question explicitly asks for them.
Scan every evidence session for answer-bearing facts before finalizing. Multi-session questions often need one fact from each session.

candidate_units.reason: blazer at dry cleaner, user still needs to pick up (clothing + store obligation)
too-small original pair to return to Zara
larger exchanged pair to pick up at Zara; distinct physical item from the returned pair
session_1 'pick up the new pair' coreferences same Zara exchange (boots, 2/5)
second mention of the same blazer dry-cleaning pickup
already owned recent purchase; no pickup/return obligation
already owned recent purchase; no pickup/return obligation
lent to sister, not a store pickup/return
owned wardrobe item under seasonal storage, not a store obligation

facts: provenance(f1, answer_afa9873b_2, "still need to pick up my dry cleaning for the navy blue blazer").
isa(zara_boots_old, clothing). attribute(zara_boots_old, store, zara). attribute(zara_boots_old, reason, too_small).
isa(zara_boots_new, clothing). attribute(zara_boots_new, store, zara). attribute(zara_boots_new, status, exchanged_larger).
provenance(f2, answer_afa9873b_3, "need to return some boots to Zara ... too small ... exchanged ... haven't picked them up").
provenance(f3, answer_afa9873b_1, "exchanged a pair of boots ... still need to pick up the new pair").
excluded(black_jeans_levis, owned). excluded(white_button_down_hm, owned). excluded(green_sweater, lent).
cases.query: answer(N) :- count_distinct(O, (action(user,O,V), member(V,[pick_up,return]), isa(O,clothing), attribute(O,store,_)), N).
cases.rationale: Three clothing items carry a store pickup/return: the navy blazer at the cleaner, the too-small Zara boots to return, and the exchanged larger boots to pick up; jeans/shirt are owned and the sweater is lent.

candidate_units.reason: user attended 'Women in Art' exhibition Feb 10 (session _1)
user volunteered/attended 'Art Afternoon' at Children's Museum Feb 17 (session _2)
user attended guided tour at History Museum Feb 24, ancient pottery/art (session _3)
user attended 'The Evolution of Street Art' lecture at Art Gallery Mar 3 (session _4)
future plan ('thinking of organizing'), not attended
assistant recommendations / upcoming festivals, not attended
planned weekend gallery visits, not attended
hypothetical future Met/Temple of Dendur visit, not attended

facts: provenance(e1, answer_901a6763_1, "Women in Art exhibition which I attended on February 10th")
provenance(e2, answer_901a6763_2, "Art Afternoon event on February 17th")
provenance(e3, answer_901a6763_3, "guided tour at the History Museum on February 24th ... ancient pottery")
attribute(e4, topic, "The Evolution of Street Art")
provenance(e4, answer_901a6763_4, "attended a lecture at the Art Gallery on 'The Evolution of Street Art' on March 3rd")
cases.query: answer(N) :- N = #count{ E : event(E,_), action(user,E,attend), isa(E,art_related) }.
cases.rationale: Four distinct user-attended art-related events, one per session (Women in Art Feb 10, Art Afternoon Feb 17, History Museum tour Feb 24, Street Art lecture Mar 3); workshops, festivals, studio visits, and the planned Met trip are excluded as future/recommended, not attended.

candidate_units.reason: got from nursery two weeks ago — plant acquired within last month
bought with peace lily at nursery two weeks ago — within last month
got from sister last month — acquisition within last month
same snake plant, fertilizer topic in session _3
same peace lily, adjustment topic in session _3
pre-existing care/misting topic; no acquisition in window
pruned a month ago, not acquired
fertilizer question; no acquisition stated
humidity care topic; no acquisition stated
repotting topic; no acquisition in window
pre-existing balcony plant; no acquisition in window

facts: provenance(f1, answer_c2204106_2, "got from the nursery two weeks ago along with a succulent").
provenance(f2, answer_c2204106_2, "a succulent plant two weeks ago").
provenance(f3, answer_c2204106_1, "my snake plant, which I got from my sister last month").
cases.query: answer(N) :- N = #count{ P : action(user, P, acquired), isa(P, plant), within_last_month(P) }.
cases.rationale: Three plants acquired within the last month — peace lily and succulent (nursery, two weeks ago) and snake plant (from sister, last month); all other plants are pre-existing care/prune/repot topics.

candidate_units.reason: Billie Eilish album user downloaded on Spotify (acquired)
Whiskey Wanderers EP user bought at merch booth; fictional-but-authoritative per contract
same EP, restated in session 2
same EP, restated in session 3
user possesses/collects a signed Tame Impala vinyl; vinyl realizes an album, possession satisfies acquired
a song on Happier Than Ever, not a separate album/EP
assistant recommendations (Melodrama, Manic, etc.), not user-acquired
not albums/EPs

facts: normalized_action(user, X, acquire) :- action(user, X, download)
normalized_action(user, X, acquire) :- action(user, X, purchase)
normalized_action(user, X, acquire) :- action(user, X, possess)
cases.query: answer(N) :- N = count{ X : normalized_action(user, X, acquire), is_album_or_ep(X) }.
cases.rationale: Three distinct user-acquired releases: downloaded Happier Than Ever, purchased EP Midnight Sky (one unit across all three sessions), and the owned Tame Impala vinyl; recommendations/playlists/podcasts/the NDA track excluded.

candidate_units.reason: user made orange bitters from orange peels -> orange citrus used by user
same citrus kind (orange); user Sangria orange slices
user made classic Daiquiri with fresh lime juice
same citrus kind (lime); user Cucumber Gimlet with lime juice
user Sangria with lemon slices, made two weeks ago
only in assistant honey-syrup/garnish/gin suggestions, not user's actual use
assistant whiskey-sour recipe orange juice/bitters, not user-asserted
assistant recipe lemon juice, not user-asserted

cases.query: answer(N) :- N = #count{ C : isa(C, citrus), used_in_user_cocktail(C) }.
cases.rationale: User actually used orange (homemade bitters + Sangria), lime (Daiquiri + Cucumber Gimlet), and lemon (Sangria); grapefruit and other citrus appear only in assistant suggestions, yielding 3 distinct citrus types.

candidate_units.reason: User tried an Ethiopian restaurant and is cooking misir wot/injera (session _4)
User learned chicken tikka masala in an Indian cuisine class (session _2)
User tried a Korean bibimbap recipe from the cooking class (session _3)
User attended a vegan cuisine class and made vegan lasagna (session _1)
Same cuisine class as the Ethiopian restaurant
Kimchi is Korean; same cuisine class as bibimbap
Fermentation is a technique, not a cuisine; sauerkraut is a single dish not framed as a learned cuisine, and its kimchi is already Korean
Japanese/teriyaki/etc. are assistant recommendations, not user-performed actions

cases.query: answer(N) :- N = #count{ C : semantic_type(C), action(user,O,V), member(V,[learned_to_cook,tried_out,attended]), isa(O,C) }.
cases.rationale: User learned-to-cook or tried four distinct cuisines — Ethiopian, Indian, Korean, and vegan — excluding the fermentation workshop (a technique) and assistant-suggested cuisines.

completed solo trip to New York City for five days
future planned Europe trip; not Hawaii/NYC and not yet taken
future plan duration; not Hawaii/NYC

facts: provenance(hawaii_trip, answer_60e8941a_1, "we had to plan everything out for the 10-day").
provenance(nyc_trip, answer_60e8941a_2, "New York City for five days").
cases.query: answer(N) :- duration(hawaii_trip, day, A), duration(nyc_trip, day, B), N is A + B.
cases.rationale: Hawaii family trip = 10 days plus NYC solo trip = 5 days; Europe/Paris/Rome are future plans and excluded; 10 + 5 = 15.

candidate_units.reason: user's own wedding is being planned (future), not attended
session _2: Emily wed Sarah, rooftop garden in the city; user 'just got back from' it
session _1 'cousin Emily's wedding in the city, rooftop garden' = same Emily+venue+location; roommate/friend/cousin label is a noisy attribute, not a separate occasion
session _1: cousin Rachel's vineyard wedding in August, user was a bridesmaid
session _3: friend Jen wed Tom at a rustic barn last weekend
'been to a few weddings recently' is a vague mention; only the named ones are distinct countable units

cases.query: answer(N) :- N = #count{ W : event(W, wedding), action(user, W, attended), not attribute(W, status, planned_future) }.
cases.rationale: Three distinct attended weddings (Emily+Sarah rooftop/city, Rachel's vineyard, Jen+Tom barn); user's own is future, and the cross-session 'cousin Emily' rooftop mention merges into the Emily wedding.
